question: Which statement about properties under `camunda.client.worker.override.<job-type>` is FALSE?

options.text: The key of the override is the job type, so each override applies only to that specific worker.
Setting `enabled=false` disables the matching job worker.
`max-retries` caps the number of retries before automatic complete, fail, or BPMN error responses are no longer attempted.
The default value of `auto-complete` for every worker is `true` whenever the override is present.

a.text: The docs state explicitly that the key of the override is the job type, so this is true and is not the false statement.

b.text: `enabled` under the override toggles the matching job worker on or off, so this is correctly stated.

c.text: `max-retries` is documented as the maximum number of retries before automatic responses (complete, fail, BPMN error) for jobs are no longer attempted, so this is true.

d.text: This is the false statement: the documented default for every property under `camunda.client.worker.override.<job-type>`, including `auto-complete`, is `null`. The override only takes effect when you explicitly set a value.

explanation: Every property listed under `camunda.client.worker.override.<job-type>` has `null` as its default value in the reference. An override applies only when explicitly set; it does not silently force `auto-complete` (or any other field) to `true` just by existing.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - properties reference
